headlines.title: Violent clashes as Albania’s anti-government protests continue
headlines.snippet: Violent clashes continue in Albania as anti-government protests persist.

headlines.title: Trump administration renews pressure on International Criminal Court
headlines.snippet: The US is increasing pressure on the International Criminal Court, stating it will reject any court attempts to assert authority over its citizens. This action follows recent judicial moves against US sanctions.

headlines.title: Nine Buddhist monks killed by child driving truck in Thailand
headlines.snippet: Nine Buddhist monks were killed in Thailand when a truck, driven by an 11-year-old boy, struck a pilgrimage procession.

headlines.title: The last time Iran held a funeral for the Supreme Leader
headlines.snippet: An estimated 10 million mourners attended the funeral of Iran's first Supreme Leader in 1989.

headlines.title: Fears of new massacre in Sudan’s el-Obeid: What do we know?
headlines.snippet: Fears of a new massacre loom in el-Obeid, Sudan, as RSF troops besiege the city. Approximately 500,000 civilians face potential mass atrocities amid drone strikes and a stalled peace process.

headlines.title: Israeli drone attack wounds people in southern Gaza
headlines.snippet: An Israeli drone attack in Khan Younis, southern Gaza, has wounded an unspecified number of people. Gaza's ambulance emergency services reported the incident, with no further casualty details released.

headlines.title: Likely next UK PM Andy Burnham set to ban Palantir from NHS
headlines.snippet: Andy Burnham, anticipated to become the next British prime minister, is reportedly planning to remove Palantir's contracts from the National Health Service (NHS). This move is part of a broader review of the government's artificial intelligence strategy.

headlines.title: Iranian foreign minister hits back at US-led military talks in Bahrain
headlines.snippet: Iran's foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, criticized a US-led regional security meeting in Bahrain via social media. He questioned the security brought by the US Central Command (Centcom) to the region and emphasized that peace requires inclusivity and no outside interference.

headlines.title: Palestinian Journalists Syndicate condemns Israel discrediting Gaza journalists’s profession
headlines.snippet: The Palestinian Journalists Syndicate has condemned Israeli allegations that question the professional status of Palestinian journalists killed in Gaza. The syndicate views this as a campaign to discredit truth-tellers and media institutions targeted by Israel.

headlines.title: Exclusive: ICC bureau changes rules to lower threshold for Khan's removal
headlines.snippet: The International Criminal Court's (ICC) executive body has modified its rules, changing the process for removing the Chief Prosecutor Karim Khan. The vote threshold has been lowered from two stages to a single vote on a motion concerning alleged misconduct.

headlines.title: UK PM Starmer Hosts Sultan of Oman for Talks
headlines.snippet: British Prime Minister Starmer hosted the Sultan of Oman for talks.

headlines.title: Could The US Ban Pregnant Visitors From Entering?
headlines.snippet: The United States is considering a ban on pregnant visitors entering the country.

headlines.title: European allies fill most gaps left by US in NATO defense plans, top commander says

headlines.title: Germany Cracks Down on ‘Exorbitant’ Sick Leave
headlines.snippet: Germany is implementing stricter measures to address what it terms 'exorbitant' sick leave.

headlines.title: Somalia peacekeeping mission at risk as US blocks UN support
headlines.snippet: The Trump administration's decision to block UN support is jeopardizing the Somalia peacekeeping mission. Officials believe this action could lead to the end of the African Union's efforts to combat the Al-Qaeda-linked Al-Shabaab.

headlines.title: Roger Waters and Mona Miari reimagine Comfortably Numb for Gaza
headlines.snippet: Roger Waters and Mona Miari have collaborated to reimagine 'Comfortably Numb' for Gaza, discussing art as resistance and music's role during conflict.

headlines.title: Syria's top diplomat reassures Lebanese leaders on Beirut visit
headlines.snippet: Syria's top diplomat, Asaad al-Shaibani, reassured Lebanese leaders during a visit to Beirut that Syria would not intervene militarily in Lebanon. This comes as Lebanon seeks to disarm Hezbollah.

headlines.title: Sudan's army closes in on RSF stronghold in West Darfur
headlines.snippet: Sudan's army is advancing towards an RSF stronghold in West Darfur, nearing El Geneina. The RSF is reportedly redeploying fighters to reinforce its position.
